problem.title: Candy
problem.description: There are n children standing in a line. Each child is assigned a rating value given in the integer array ratings.
assumptions: Each child must have at least 1 candy.
Children with a higher rating than an adjacent neighbor get more candies than that neighbor.

example.explanation: Minimum candies: [2,1,2]. Each child must have ≥1 and children with higher ratings than neighbors get more.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pass [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Initialize every child with one candy, then make two passes: left-to-right to ensure each child with a higher rating than their left neighbor gets more candy, and right-to-left to enforce the same constraint with the right neighbor. Taking the max during the second pass preserves both constraints simultaneously.
